epic_candidate.title: [Epic] Automated FBM Order Fulfillment Pipeline for Amazon.de
epic_candidate.description: Build an end-to-end automation system that replaces manual VA morning workflows for unshipped FBM orders on Amazon.de. The system syncs orders from Amazon Seller Central, intelligently batches them by SKU quantity and brand, generates pick lists and pack slips as PDFs, produces DHL and Easy Ship shipping labels, prevents duplicate processing, and provides a staff dashboard for monitoring and intervention.

story_candidate.title: [User Story] Automated Daily FBM Order Processing Pipeline
story_candidate.story: As a Virtual Assistant or operations staff member, I want the system to automatically sync, batch, and process all unshipped FBM orders from Amazon.de each morning so that I can save hours of manual work and focus on exceptions and oversight rather than repetitive order processing.
story_candidate.benefit: Reduces daily processing time from hours to minutes, eliminates manual errors, and allows staff to focus on exception handling.

acceptance_criteria.title: Amazon.de Order Sync Completes Successfully

given_when_then.given: the system is configured with valid Amazon.de Seller Central SP-API credentials
given_when_then.when: the daily scheduled pipeline executes at the configured morning time
given_when_then.then: the system shall retrieve all unshipped FBM orders within the last 24 hours, paginating through results as needed, and log the count of retrieved orders

acceptance_criteria.verification: Check pipeline logs for successful sync completion with order count matching Amazon Seller Central dashboard
edge_cases: No unshipped orders exist — system logs '0 orders retrieved' and skips processing
API returns rate limit error — system backs off and retries up to 3 times
API token has expired — system logs authentication failure and sends notification

acceptance_criteria.title: Order Items Retrieved with German Marketplace Details

given_when_then.given: the system has retrieved a list of unshipped orders
given_when_then.then: the system shall fetch individual order items including SKU, quantity 1–n, unit price, extended price, shipping address (Str., Hausnummer, PLZ, Ort, Land), VAT rate and amount, and any seller notes

acceptance_criteria.verification: Spot-check 5 random orders against Amazon Seller Central order details page
edge_cases: Order has more than 100 line items — system fetches all pages
Shipping address contains special German characters (ä, ö, ü, ß) — preserved correctly
VAT is 0% or 19% (German standard) — handled correctly

acceptance_criteria.title: Orders Split into Correct Batch Types

given_when_then.given: the system has retrieved order items for all unshipped orders
given_when_then.then: orders shall be classified into exactly four batch types: (a) Single SKU qty 1, (b) Single SKU qty 2, (c) Single SKU qty 3+, (d) Mixed/multi-line orders with multiple distinct SKUs

acceptance_criteria.verification: For 10 test orders with known compositions, verify each is assigned to the correct batch type
edge_cases: Order with single SKU quantity 0 — excluded as invalid
Order with single SKU quantity 1 in one line, same SKU quantity 1 in another line — treated as single SKU qty 2 (consolidated)
Order with 50 distinct SKUs — correctly classified as mixed/multi-line

acceptance_criteria.title: Brand/SKU Group Segregation Applied

given_when_then.given: orders have been classified into batch types
given_when_then.then: order items within each batch type shall be grouped by brand/SKU group, and items from the same brand shall appear consecutively on pick lists

acceptance_criteria.verification: Inspect generated pick list groupings for correct brand segregation
edge_cases: Brand name contains special characters — handled correctly
Item has no brand assigned — grouped under 'Uncategorized'
Only one brand in entire batch — all items in one group

acceptance_criteria.title: Items Correctly Flagged for Review

given_when_then.given: the system has processed all order items
given_when_then.then: items matching any review rule (high-value threshold, unusual address, special handling notes, configurable rules) shall be flagged and excluded from automatic document and label generation

acceptance_criteria.verification: Configure a test review rule (e.g., value > €500), process orders, verify matching items are flagged
edge_cases: All items in an order are flagged — entire order held for review
Some items flagged, some not — only non-flagged items proceed
Review rules configuration is empty — no items flagged

acceptance_criteria.title: Pick List PDF Generated and Correct

given_when_then.given: the system has batched and segregated orders
given_when_then.then: a PDF pick list shall be generated for each batch/brand group containing: item locations, quantities to pick, order references, and batch metadata. The PDF shall be printable on A4 paper.

acceptance_criteria.verification: Open generated PDF and verify content includes all required fields; print test to confirm A4 compatibility
edge_cases: Batch has 0 items — no pick list generated (logged as 'empty batch')
Batch has 500+ items — pick list spans multiple pages with page numbers
Item location data missing — shows 'Location not set'

acceptance_criteria.title: Pack Slip PDF Generated Per Order

given_when_then.given: the system has prepared orders for fulfillment
given_when_then.then: a PDF pack slip shall be generated for each order containing: item details with quantities, customer shipping address in German format (Name, Straße, PLZ Ort, Land), order ID, date, and a barcode/QR code for tracking

acceptance_criteria.verification: Verify pack slip PDF contains correct item details and German-formatted address for 5 test orders
edge_cases: Shipping address contains a Packstation or Postfach — format preserved correctly
Order has 1 item — pack slip still generated as single-page PDF
Customer name contains umlauts — displayed correctly in PDF

acceptance_criteria.title: Duplicate SKU Consolidation

given_when_then.given: an order contains multiple line items for the same SKU
given_when_then.when: generating pick lists and pack slips
given_when_then.then: the system shall display the SKU once with the total consolidated quantity rather than as separate lines

acceptance_criteria.verification: Create test order with 3 line items of the same SKU (qty 1 each), verify pick list shows 1 line with qty 3
edge_cases: Same SKU appears across different batch types — handled within each batch independently
Same SKU with different prices per line — consolidated but notes the price variance

acceptance_criteria.title: DHL Shipping Labels Generated as Batched PDF

given_when_then.given: orders are ready for shipping and DHL is the selected carrier
given_when_then.when: the DHL label generation API is called
given_when_then.then: the system shall generate DHL shipping labels for each eligible order, produce them as individual PDFs, and compile a batched multi-page PDF for bulk printing

acceptance_criteria.verification: Verify DHL label PDFs are valid (can be opened), check batch PDF contains all labels in order
edge_cases: DHL API is unavailable — system retries 3 times with backoff, then logs error and notifies staff
Order shipping address is outside DHL service area — system attempts Easy Ship or flags for manual handling
DHL label generation partially fails (e.g., 8/10 succeed) — success and failure both logged

acceptance_criteria.title: Easy Ship Labels Generated as Batched PDF

given_when_then.given: orders are ready for shipping and Easy Ship is the selected carrier
given_when_then.when: the Easy Ship label generation API is called
given_when_then.then: the system shall generate Easy Ship shipping labels for each eligible order, produce them as individual PDFs, and compile a batched multi-page PDF for bulk printing

acceptance_criteria.verification: Verify Easy Ship label PDFs are valid, check batch PDF contents
edge_cases: Easy Ship API returns quota exceeded error — queue remaining and retry after delay
Order dimensions/weight exceed Easy Ship limits — flag for manual handling

acceptance_criteria.title: PDF Batches Organized Logically

given_when_then.given: the system generates multiple PDF documents
given_when_then.then: batches shall be organized by type (pick lists, pack slips, DHL labels, Easy Ship labels) and further subdivided by brand group or carrier as appropriate. Each batch PDF shall have a cover page with summary metadata.

acceptance_criteria.verification: Review generated batch folders and verify logical organization
edge_cases: Only one document in a batch — still wrapped in batch PDF with cover sheet
Multiple carriers used on same day — separate batch PDFs per carrier

acceptance_criteria.title: Staff Dashboard Shows Current Status

given_when_then.given: a VA or operations staff member navigates to the dashboard URL
given_when_then.then: the dashboard shall display: pending orders count, flagged items requiring review, processing status (idle/running/completed/failed), generated documents summary, daily processing summary with timestamps, and quick actions for authorized users

acceptance_criteria.verification: Login as read-only user, verify all sections load; login as admin, verify quick actions visible
edge_cases: No processing has run today — dashboard shows 'No data for today' with yesterday's summary
Dashboard loads while pipeline is running — shows real-time progress indicators
User has JavaScript disabled — basic HTML fallback shows key metrics

acceptance_criteria.title: Logging Captures All Operations

given_when_then.then: logs shall contain: timestamp, operation type, order IDs affected, result (success/failure), duration, and error details for failures. Logs shall be searchable by date range, operation type, and order ID.

acceptance_criteria.verification: Execute pipeline, query logs for each major operation and verify completeness
edge_cases: Log retention exceeds 90 days — oldest logs archived or rotated
Disk space low — system warns but continues processing
Multiple pipelines run same day — logs distinguishable by run ID

acceptance_criteria.title: Error Handling and Notifications Work

given_when_then.given: a pipeline operation encounters a failure
given_when_then.then: the system shall: (a) log the error with full details, (b) retry up to 3 times with exponential backoff, (c) if all retries fail, send a notification to configured staff (email or other channel), and (d) continue processing remaining items where possible

acceptance_criteria.verification: Simulate an API failure (e.g., invalid credentials), verify retry behavior and notification delivery
edge_cases: Critical failure at start of pipeline — pipeline aborts with notification
Transient failure mid-pipeline — retry succeeds, pipeline continues
Notification delivery fails — logged as separate error event

acceptance_criteria.title: Daily Scheduled Execution with Manual Trigger

given_when_then.given: the system is configured with a daily schedule (e.g., 06:00 CET)
given_when_then.then: the pipeline shall start automatically without manual intervention. Additionally, an authorized user shall be able to trigger the pipeline manually via the dashboard at any time.

acceptance_criteria.verification: Set schedule to 2 minutes from now, verify auto-start; trigger manually via dashboard, verify execution
edge_cases: Manual trigger while pipeline is already running — second trigger rejected with 'Pipeline already running' message
Scheduled time missed (system was down) — catch-up logic triggers on restart
Schedule configuration invalid — system uses default schedule and logs warning

non_functional_acceptance_criteria.title: Pipeline Completes Within 15 Minutes for 500 Orders
non_functional_acceptance_criteria.description: With 500 test orders in the Amazon.de account, the full pipeline from sync to label generation shall complete within 15 minutes.
non_functional_acceptance_criteria.verification: Time the pipeline execution end-to-end with 500 mock orders. Repeat 3 times and take average.

non_functional_acceptance_criteria.title: Zero Data Loss Across Runs
non_functional_acceptance_criteria.description: Every unshipped order at the start of a pipeline run shall either be processed to completion or explicitly flagged for review. No order shall silently disappear.
non_functional_acceptance_criteria.verification: Compare order count from Amazon API before run vs. processed+flagged count after run. Must match exactly.
non_functional_acceptance_criteria.pass_fail_criteria: 100% match between source orders and processed+flagged orders

non_functional_acceptance_criteria.title: Credentials Encrypted at Rest
non_functional_acceptance_criteria.description: All API credentials stored in the system's configuration database shall be encrypted using AES-256 or equivalent. Plain-text credentials must never appear in logs, error messages, or configuration exports.
non_functional_acceptance_criteria.verification: Inspect credential storage mechanism; grep logs for credential patterns after a full pipeline run
non_functional_acceptance_criteria.pass_fail_criteria: No plain-text credentials found in any log or config export

non_functional_acceptance_criteria.title: German Address Format Correct
non_functional_acceptance_criteria.description: All addresses on generated documents shall follow German format: Empfänger, Straße Hausnummer, PLZ Ort, Deutschland. Special characters (ä, ö, ü, ß, ß) shall display correctly.
non_functional_acceptance_criteria.verification: Generate documents for 10 German addresses covering edge cases (Packstation, c/o, multiple recipients)

non_functional_acceptance_criteria.title: GDPR Data Retention Enforced
non_functional_acceptance_criteria.description: Customer personal data (names, addresses) shall be automatically purged or anonymized 90 days after order fulfillment completion.
non_functional_acceptance_criteria.verification: Insert test data with known date, advance system clock past 90 days, verify data is purged
non_functional_acceptance_criteria.pass_fail_criteria: Test customer data not retrievable after 90 + 1 day

non_functional_acceptance_criteria.title: Role-Based Access Control Enforced
non_functional_acceptance_criteria.description: Read-only users shall only view dashboard data. Admin users shall additionally access pipeline controls and configuration.
non_functional_acceptance_criteria.verification: Login as read-only user — verify pipeline trigger button is hidden/disabled. Login as admin — verify button is active.
non_functional_acceptance_criteria.pass_fail_criteria: Read-only user cannot trigger pipeline via UI or API

definition_of_done: All 15 functional acceptance criteria pass verification
All 6 non-functional acceptance criteria pass verification
Pipeline processes 500 test orders within 15 minutes
Zero data loss confirmed over 5 consecutive test runs
PDF documents are valid and printable on A4
DHL and Easy Ship label generation confirmed with sandbox/test APIs
Logs capture all operations with searchable fields
Error notifications deliver correctly to configured channels
Dashboard displays correctly for both read-only and admin roles
German address formatting confirmed correct for all edge cases

unit_tests: Batch splitting algorithm with all four batch type scenarios
Brand segregation logic with edge cases
integration_tests: Amazon SP-API sync with pagination and rate limiting
DHL API label generation with success and failure scenarios
Easy Ship API label generation with success and failure scenarios
manual_tests: Full end-to-end pipeline with real Amazon.de seller account (sandbox)
PDF visual inspection for all document types
Dashboard UX flow for read-only and admin users
Print test for A4 compatibility of all PDFs
German address special character rendering in PDFs

task_issue_candidates.title: [Task] Amazon.de SP-API Integration Module
task_issue_candidates.description: Implement the authentication and order synchronization module for Amazon.de Seller Central SP-API including OAuth flow, paginated order retrieval, order item fetching, and rate limit handling.

task_issue_candidates.title: [Task] Order Processing Engine - Batching & Segregation
task_issue_candidates.description: Implement the core order processing engine that classifies orders into batch types, segregates by brand/SKU group, applies review rules, and consolidates duplicate SKUs.

task_issue_candidates.title: [Task] PDF Document Generation (Pick Lists & Pack Slips)
task_issue_candidates.description: Implement PDF generation for pick lists (organized by batch/brand) and pack slips (per order with German addresses, barcodes). Include batch PDF compilation with cover sheets.

task_issue_candidates.title: [Task] DHL Shipping Integration Module
task_issue_candidates.description: Implement DHL shipping API integration for label generation including authentication, label request/retrieval, PDF generation, batch compilation, and error handling.

task_issue_candidates.title: [Task] Easy Ship Shipping Integration Module
task_issue_candidates.description: Implement Easy Ship shipping API integration for label generation including authentication, label request/retrieval, PDF generation, batch compilation, and error handling.

task_issue_candidates.title: [Task] Staff Dashboard Web UI
task_issue_candidates.description: Build a simple web-based dashboard for VAs and operations staff showing pipeline status, order metrics, flagged items, document summaries, and administrative controls with RBAC.

task_issue_candidates.title: [Task] Pipeline Scheduler & Orchestrator
task_issue_candidates.description: Implement the pipeline orchestration layer including scheduled execution, manual triggers, state management (idle/running/completed/failed), and catch-up logic.

task_issue_candidates.title: [Task] Logging, Monitoring & Error Handling Infrastructure
task_issue_candidates.description: Implement comprehensive logging infrastructure, error handling with retry logic, health check endpoints, notification delivery (email/Slack), and log search capabilities.

task_issue_candidates.title: [Task] Configuration & Secrets Management
task_issue_candidates.description: Implement configuration management (schedule, thresholds, carrier defaults) and secure secrets storage for API credentials with encryption at rest.
